programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A man who died after being knocked down by a van is revealed to be a professional hitman responsible for at least six contract killings - and his next target was to be a navy lieutenant commander. Abby is delighted at being a match for a teenager who needs a kidney transplant, but she is informed that someone else with very similar DNA has also been identified as suitable. Has she found a relative she never knew about?

programme.desc: Maddy puts her career at risk when she agrees to secretly treat a stabbing victim as a favour for her sister, who has recently been released from prison. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harassed Harry is forced to close the department to members of the public after a prison riot leaves several inmates injured, and the undue stress he's under causes him to lash out at Lara, blaming her for Tally's predicament. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy encounters Ryan Jones again and finds the battered corpse of his wife Connie in the boot of his car. As the murderer makes his getaway in the London Underground, it soon becomes clear he has a personal vendetta against the Sun Hill desk sergeant and plans further killings, working his way through Smithy's address book - beginning with his mother Pauline. Elsewhere, Gino accidentally finds and reads Ann's novel, and Elizabeth is invited to a function in Sydney by Douglas Goddard

programme.title: Peak Practice

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean calls on Alistair's help to do a spot of match-making and Lionel finds himself in the middle of a muddle. Comedy, with Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth and Richard finally move into their new country retreat, and while she thinks it is 'olde worlde bijou', he finds it a decided disappointment. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth press-gangs a reluctant Richard into helping repair the lights at the local church hall. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes
programme.desc: It is 1931 and socialite Iris Carr (Tuppence Middleton) is travelling back from a holiday in the Balkans, but faints on the platform of the railway station in the scorching heat. She is comforted by fellow passenger Miss Froy (Selina Cadell), but when Iris falls asleep she awakes to find the woman has disappeared and everyone else on board denies ever having seen her. With only handsome traveller Max Hare (Tom Hughes) for support, Iris maintains that Miss Froy has somehow been abducted - even though it increasingly appears to be a delusion on her part. Mystery, also starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones, Stephanie Cole and Julian Rhind-Tutt. Adapted from Ethel Lina White's novel, The Wheel Spins

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The unsolved-crime squad reopens the case of an Asian woman who was attacked eight years ago, and has been in a coma ever since. After enlisting the aid of a community liaison officer, it becomes apparent there is conflict in the victim's family, as well as a possible connection with the disappearance of a private detective. Nina Wadia, Keith Allen and Navin Chowdhry guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Russian sleeper agents plan to detonate a nuclear bomb in London, leaving the team with no alternative but to kidnap the mole and force her to help prevent the attack. The trail leads the pair to an exclusive club where Havers goes undercover to delve beneath the respectable surface and find the killer. Jenny Agutter guest stars, with Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.desc: Judith's former boyfriend Alistair rocks the boat by asking Sandy for a date, and Jean is given cause to feel jealous of Lionel. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.desc: Hyacinth volunteers to help with an OAP day-trip to the seaside, but her plans bring nothing but trouble, landing her in the arms of an amorous Italian pensioner. Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift star

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth prepares for the Mayor's annual fancy-dress ball, only to have her dream of wearing a regal costume shattered. programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The agents are in a state of panic following Harry's kidnapping and their worst fears are confirmed when footage of his killing is posted on the internet, but Ros and Lucas refuse to accept their leader is dead and mount a mission to identify his captors. The discovery of his abductor's body destroys their strongest lead, and they turn to the new CIA liaison officer for help. However, the unexpected return of a former colleague holds the key to Harry's whereabouts and an international conspiracy. Drama, starring Hermione Norris and Richard Armitage

programme.desc: The discovery of a concrete-encased body at a Docklands building site leads to conflict between DCI Hunt and the location's owner. Meanwhile, Alex discovers that her fascination with the case is distracting her from returning home

programme.desc: Gene learns crooked cops are going to interrupt a bullion robbery and steal the gold for themselves - a discovery that gives him the chance to root out corruption in the force once and for all. Tina and Shirley are eager to see Ian's downfall, Phil and Sharon's divorce gets ugly and a furious Ben will stop at nothing to find out who informed on the Mitchells

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets the Sweet family in Weston-super-Mare, whose three children have all been diagnosed with multiple rare illnesses and will soon be full-time wheelchair-users. It may also affect their speech, fine motor skills and vision. All three children already struggle with basic normal life at home, despite the love and care of their parents Cat and Chris. So the DIY SOS team - plus hundreds of local workers - are here to give them the four walls they need

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals

programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win a £10,000 jackpot, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a pair of returning expats find their ideal country home in North Wales for a budget of £350,000. Away from hunt, the presenter heads out with a local fisherman to gather wild mussels using an ancient method that has been used on Wales' northern coast for 200 years

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues more items from a recycling centre in Altrincham. He turns to woodworker Bruce Faulseit to restyle a discarded pub table, while furniture restorer Katie Keat is tasked with bringing a mid-century table back to its best. A tired badminton supplies box is Jay's creative project, but will he succeed in turning trash into cash?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea is delighted to work on a 1950s toy bike gifted to the owner's brother by comedy duo Laurel and Hardy, during a performance in their home town. The pedals have seized up and rust has taken hold of the body work. Other items worked on include a pair of beloved boxing gloves and a shattered ostrich egg cigarette box. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic great Hannah Cockcroft, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey, Olympic hockey champion Kate Richardson-Walsh and Toronto Wolfpack forward Jon Wilkin join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker for the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It seems everyone is out to get Ian, with Max planning his revenge and an angry Ben confronting his brother. Just then the police arrive and Ben presumes they are here to speak to him - but Ian is the one they arrest. Bobby starts to see his dad for who he really is, and Mick tells Tina that his depression is nothing to do with losing the Vic

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: The level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows as comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return for a third time to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts the quiz, inviting author and presenter Dr Hannah Fry and stand-up comedian Phil Wang to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: The wedding is put at risk after Denise kicks up a stink and accuses Dave of a heinous crime - flirting with Beverley Macca. As far as she's concerned, the wedding is off. After a talk with parents Jim and Barbara, Denise softens her stance and the family seeks an appropriate way to celebrate the pair's reconciliation. Comedy written by and starring Caroline Aherne and Craig Cash, with Ricky Tomlinson and Sue Johnston

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets actor and writer Stephen Fry, who is promoting Troy, his latest reworking of the Greek myths and Mamma Mia star Amanda Seyfried discusses new David Fincher film Mank. Back to the Future star and Parkinson's disease activist Michael J Fox chats about his memoir No Time Like the Future: An Optimist Considers Mortality, Nina Sosanya talks about her new Christmas drama, TV chef Jamie Oliver explains how his new book 7 Ways manages to breathe new life into staple ingredients and comedian Tom Allen talks about his memoir No Shame. Plus, Dolly Parton makes a special appearance performing the seaonal staple I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The Drag Race UK tour reaches Birmingham. The clock strikes 8pm and the fans are ready for their girls. The lights are down, the curtain is up, but the queens or nowhere to be seen. Will a miscue spell disaster?

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The cold and flu season has the queens struggling to make it through the tour. First, Divina succumbs then Blu, with Sum Ting - who's been battling a cough since the start - losing her voice. With no backing tracks available, it remains to be seen whether she be able to pull off her solo

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a brand new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize. Each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by special guest Tim Peake. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes featuring one of her favourite ingredients - the anchovy. She prepares a creamy dressing for the fish, served with bitter leaves and walnuts, and then creates an elixir, combining silvery salted anchovies with garlic and oil. This not only makes an excellent dip for red chicory, but also the perfect accompaniment to a platter of sliced seared steak. Nigella creates an anchovy pasta dish of spaghetti with chard and chilli, and also makes a lemon blossom cocktail and a lemon and elderflower pudding

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Chelsea v Leeds United and Liverpool v Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the opening semi-final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. After facing Liang Wenbo in the quarter-final, China's Ding Junhui played another of his compatriots at this stage last year, defeating Yan Bingtao to book his place in the final against Scot Stephen Maguire. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special 1990s edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Tamara Beckwith, Saffron Sprackling, Clare Buckfield, John Pickard, Mr Motivator, Diane Youdale, Nick Moran and Vas Blackwood

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Ali and Nevada from London, engaged couple Sean and Alan from Chester, and military drummer Ronnie and his partner Wesley from Aldershot

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: It's Musicals Week on Strictly, meaning that remaining contestants Bill, HRVY, Jamie, JJ, Maisie and Ranvir - minus Clara, who was voted off last week - are keen to dazzle as perform routines inspired by hit shows including Little Shop of Horrors, Phantom of the Opera, Chitty Chitty Bang Bang and A Chorus Line. However, it's also the quarter-final and with the final looming ever closer, the pressure is on. The extravaganza gets under way with a Priscilla-themed routine from the professionals. Presented Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize. Each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund receives a visit from the bloodthirsty Bishop of Bath and Wells, who threatens to do him a mischief with a red-hot poker over the matter of an unpaid loan. As he attempts to raise the £1,000 he needs, he finds himself being constantly summoned to the court by the Queen. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Chelsea v Leeds United at Stamford Bridge, Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium and Burnley v Everton at Turf Moor

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Baltimore Ravens v Dallas Cowboys at M&T Bank Stadium

programme.title: Keeping Rosy (2014)
programme.desc: A businesswoman who has devoted her life to running a media agency is betrayed by her partners. Emotional and angry, she commits an uncharacteristically violent act, and takes increasingly desperate measures to make amends and protect an innocent child from further harm. Thriller, starring Maxine Peake and Blake Harrison

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a Victorian house in London's Crystal Palace, a chalet bungalow in Rackheath near Norwich, and a terrace house in Canterbury

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades salvages three items from Walsall Recycling Centre in the West Midlands, restoring and repairing retro tulip chairs, some old fabric and a bureau

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon Reeve investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the latest last-16 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Ding Junhui edged out seven-time champion Ronnie O'Sullivan 6-5 as he continued his march to the title at this stage last year. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team visits the University of Nottingham, where antique expert Caroline Hawley is wowed by a huge Shakespearean-themed quilt and Charles Hanson comes across an 18th-century handwritten Valentine that is hotly contested at the auction. Presenter Paul Martin visits a gorge in north Nottinghamshire called Creswell Crags, where in 2003 prehistoric cave art was discovered

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Weekday fanzine devoted to the ballroom dancing show, featuring interviews with the couples and a sneak peek at what they will be wearing on Saturday with designer Vicky Gill

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the concluding last-16 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. At this stage last year, Liang Wenbo prevailed 6-4 in an all-Chinese match, and John Higgins won by the same scoreline against fellow former world champion Stuart Bingham. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Woody the St Bernard moves in with five-year-old Dexter, who has cerebral palsy. Dexter's family, in Surrey, are hoping that the little pup will become his companion and learn to carry out simple assistance tasks. In Hull, Mungo joins Alastair, Daron and their possessive older cocker spaniel Milo - but the dogs clash from day one, and Alastair and Daron call on Louise to help

programme.title: New Elizabethans with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: Documentary following the rigid, class-obsessed Britain of the 1950s to the more liberal, inclusive society in the latter part of the Queen's reign. Andrew Marr begins by taking in the liberal victories of the 'permissive society' - as well as the ferocious backlash of middle England at the perceived erosion of family values. He also looks at people's attitudes to movie starlet Diana Dors and wannabe movie starlet Ruth Ellis - who achieved fame of a completely different kind

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Maff Brown, Ria Lina, Ed Byrne and Rhys James. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and comedy correspondents Rachel Parris, Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on everything that happened in the week the programme first aired in June 2019

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents action from the day's last-16 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship Extra
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents extended highlights of one of the day's best last-16 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: A light-hearted look at the social and cultural changes society went through since the turn of the 21st century, featuring contributions from politicians and celebrities. In the fourth episode, Angela Scanlon, Nigel Harman and Rachel Parris take a light-hearted look back at the year 2003, from Beyonce's hit Crazy in Love, to David Blaine living in a box over the Thames

programme.title: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Fall of an Icon
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the Myanmar leader's fall from grace. While under house arrest during the country's military dictatorship, she became a Nobel prize-winning champion of democracy, human rights and peaceful protest. However, since becoming the leader of her nation, she has been implicated in brutal atrocities against the Rohingya Muslims, and faced allegations of genocide at the International Court of Justice. Key figures in international politics discuss the reasons why the qualities that brought her such acclaim have been in marked contrast to the realities of her leadership

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin catches up with an ambitious project to create a forever home in Crewe. Plus, a former stables in Derby and a studio flat in London's Paddington

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles finds out about the history of the humble spoon

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Silversmith Brenton West takes a look at a brass replica fire engine, while Andrew Gorrie from Lancashire hopes leatherwork expert Suzie Fletcher can save a fragile leather wallet with an extraordinary history. Elsewhere, the experts set to work repairing a handmade radio system and a banana barrow

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the opening two quarter-finals from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Eventual champion Ding Junhui prevailed 6-2 in an all-Chinese clash with Liang Wenbo, and another of his compatriots was confirmed as his semi-final opponent when Yan Bingtao prevailed 6-3 against Scotland's John Higgins. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Penrhyn Castle in north Wales, experts Adam Partridge and David Harper come across a rare first edition of Dracula and a colourful Victorian watercolour set. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin gets to try his hand at splitting slate while learning about one of Wales' oldest industries

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the week's ultimate winner finally declared

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: All the backstage gossip from Strictly Come Dancing, as the contenders prepare for tomorrow night's show. Including reports from the studio rehearsals and interviews with celebrity fans

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the concluding two quarter-finals from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Nigel Bond's surprise run came to an end at this stage last year when he was edged out 6-5 by Mark Allen, and Stephen Maguire prevailed 6-4 against Matthew Stevens. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Waterhole: Africa's Animal Oasis
programme.desc: Waterholes are vital to the African ecosystem, but little is known about how they support so much life. Chris Packham and Ella Al-Shamahi work with Mwiba Wildlife Reserve in Tanzania to build the world's first waterhole with a built-in specialist camera rig

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: A look back at the year in music, featuring some of the most popular songs of 1988 as well as interviews with those who performed them, including Matt Goss and Jason Donovan. Narrated by Mel Giedroyc

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Faisal Islam, the BBC's economics editor, presents an analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents action from the quarter-finals at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part two of two. As the warring Battlestars Pegasus and Galactica prepare to combine forces for an attack on the 'Resurrection' ship, members of a group of Cylon sympathisers appear, demanding humanity broker peace with their enemy and using violent sabotage to deliver their message. Meanwhile, Apollo faces a momentous decision and Baltar takes an interest in Gina

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Cylon sympathisers begin a campaign of sabotage and destruction in the fleet, demanding peace with the enemy. Roslin lies on her deathbed, flashing back to her life on Caprica. Recalling her complicated relationship with the former president, she demands the abortion of Sharon's hybrid child. Gaius meets Gina and begins to find his loyalties divided

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Richard Bilton reports on the businesses fighting to survive the pandemic as customers are left unable to make use of their services, from the adventure playground with no children to an events company with no events. Filmed over the course of the first and second lockdowns, this programme reveals the pressure on those trying to save jobs and livelihoods.

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson play video clips featuring a mysterious web that covered an entire street, and an Australian sea turned into a foam bath

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC mates embark on a Vietnamese river adventure, where they make paper using an ancient technique and take part in a Bollywood extravaganza

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield create a wheelchair-accessible old-fashioned cinema den full of Hollywood glamour for 11-year-old movie buff Lola and her mother

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Children's magazine with information, topical items and performance

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: From slow-worms to foxes, naturalist Steve Backshall explores deadly animals found in gardens

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Steve Backshall turns detective to solve CBBC viewers' animal mysteries. From birds to bees, Steve answers questions about flying animals

programme.title: Waterhole: Africa's Animal Oasis
programme.desc: Waterholes are vital to the African ecosystem, but little is known about how they support so much life. Chris Packham and Ella Al-Shamahi work with Mwiba Wildlife Reserve in Tanzania to build the world's first waterhole with a built-in specialist camera rig

programme.title: Robot Wars: Battle of the Stars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the first of two specials in which celebrity teams led by the Brownlee brothers, Scott Mills, Dr Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Suzi Perry design their dream robot with the help of an experienced engineer, then battle it out in the arena - where all the same rules apply for the celebrities as they face off against each other and the imposing house robots. Who will become the Celebrity Robot Wars champions?

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Woody the St Bernard moves in with five-year-old Dexter, who has cerebral palsy. Dexter's family, in Surrey, are hoping that the little pup will become his companion and learn to carry out simple assistance tasks. In Hull, Mungo joins Alastair, Daron and their possessive older cocker spaniel Milo - but the dogs clash from day one, and Alastair and Daron call on Louise to help

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King create festive recipes and chat to celebrity guests, with the Rev Kate Bottley helping them make cherry and bourbon-glazed gammon

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams search for well-priced antiques and collectibles to sell at auction in the streets of Hungerford, Berkshire, with the assistance of experts Philip Serrell and Richard Madley

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves three things about to be dumped at a tip, transforms them into bespoke and valuable pieces, before selling them and giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Hampshire with a dynamic couple who love the outdoors and have £650,000 to buy a property and, while in the county, visits the Solent Sky Museum

programme.title: The Railway Children (1970)
programme.desc: Three Edwardian children are forced to leave their comfortable middle-class London home and move to the wilds of Yorkshire after their father is wrongfully imprisoned for treason. Family drama adapted from E Nesbit's story, starring Jenny Agutter, Sally Thomsett, Gary Warren, Dinah Sheridan, Bernard Cribbins, William Mervyn and Iain Cuthbertson

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents further coverage of the opening semi-final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. This is the first time the competition has been held outside York since 2010, when John Higgins prevailed in Telford. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Porridge (1979)
programme.desc: Cellmates Fletcher and Godber are coerced into aiding an escape attempt organised by the sinister Grouty. However, the two jailbirds contrive to break back into prison before their absence is detected by the formidable Mr Mackay. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale, Peter Vaughan, Fulton Mackay and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the second semi-final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Scotland's Stephen Maguire was in dominant form at this stage last year, as he thrashed Northern Ireland's Mark Allen 6-0 to reach the final of this tournament for the third time in his career and the first since 2007. However, as was the case 12 years earlier, he did not manage to add to his 2004 title, losing to Ding Junhui in the showpiece match. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Dirty God (2019)
programme.desc: Jade, a young mother in the prime of her life, is left severely burned by an acid attack. While her face has been reconstructed, her beauty is lost beneath the scars. Descending a self-destructive path with relationships crumbling, Jade must take drastic action to reclaim her life. Drama, starring Vicky Knight and Katherine Kelly

programme.title: Louis Theroux: My Scientology Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The reporter investigates the controversial religious organisation, but finds himself denied access to anyone involved. Adopting a different approach, he resorts to recruiting actors to re-enact the personal stories of former members - only to find his project is under surveillance by representatives of the church

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: An examination of the major advances made with the invention of the steam turbine, which is still used today to generate electricity in coal-fired and nuclear power stations. Fred also discovers how the country's steam heritage is preserved by museums and societies throughout the country

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an image of crashing waves in the moonlight, and demonstrates what can be achieved when painting on to a black canvas

programme.title: High Society (1956)
programme.desc: Romantic complications arise when a playboy composer sets out to win back his ex-wife. However, she is about to marry another man - and the situation becomes even more difficult when a cynical reporter enters the fray. Musical comedy based on The Philadelphia Story with a score by Cole Porter, starring Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Grace Kelly, with Louis Armstrong appearing as himself

programme.title: A Little Later
programme.desc: Musical highlights from Later with Jools Holland, featuring performances by Smokey Robinson, Carole King, Gladys Knight and Tom Jones

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the artist over the course of the last year to chart her creative process at work, and talks about her life to date and how she sees the rest of her career. Tracey discusses her troubled early years in Margate and the series of breakthroughs in the 1990s that saw her become a leading light of the Young British Artists, featuring career-defining work like My Bed and her embroidered tent Everyone I Have Ever Slept With 1963-1995. Contributors include Sir Nicholas Serota, Jay Jopling, Maria Balshaw and David Dawson

programme.title: What a Picture: Mary Whitehouse
programme.desc: Vintage interview in which Morgan Cross talks to Mary Whitehouse, President of the National Viewers and Listeners Association, who shares memories from her photo albums

programme.title: Inside Cinema
programme.desc: Film critic Catherine Bray hosts a celebration of cinematic guilty pleasures, from Showgirls and Love Actually, to Cats and The Room

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an image of crashing waves in the moonlight, and demonstrates what can be achieved when painting on to a black canvas

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: An examination of the major advances made with the invention of the steam turbine, which is still used today to generate electricity in coal-fired and nuclear power stations. Fred also discovers how the country's steam heritage is preserved by museums and societies throughout the country

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Film-maker Xavier Alford talks to other people who have Guillain-Barre syndrome as he attempts to come to terms with being diagnosed as having the condition, which the director had been hiding from family and close friends. He is confronted with the seriousness of the illness when some of the patients he meets are on the edge of life, locked in their own bodies, completely paralysed and unable to move a single muscle, while their brains remain unaffected

programme.title: The Truth About Christmas Carols
programme.desc: Composer Howard Goodall uncovers the often turbulent and uncomfortable relationship between carols and the celebration of Christmas. Though around since the 12th century, the hymns have only been a feature of services for the past 150 years, as the Church believed them to be pagan or of no value. They have been used as secret codes, saved from the brink of extinction and subject to state censorship. The programme includes performances by folk star Bella Hardy, the Purbeck Village Choir and the Truro Cathedral Choir, who recreate the very first Service of Nine Lessons and Carols

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on April 26, with performances by artists including Pat & Mick, Natalie Cole, Unique 3, Paula Abdul, Jesus Jones, Phil Collins, Tongue ‘n' Cheek, Madonna and Bruce Dickinson

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on May 3, with performances by artists including Sinitta, Soul II Soul, Morrissey, the Adventures of Stevie V, BBG featuring Dina Taylor, Bruce Dickinson, Adamski, Madonna and Emma

programme.title: The Sound of TV with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer addresses the history and use of music in television, beginning by examining the enduring power of the theme tune. On the streets of Coronation Street, he encounters a brass band playing the music that has announced the start of each episode since it began. Following the trail of the soap opera world, Neil meets composer Simon May, creator of the EastEnders theme tune, and travels to the US to talk with Matt Groening, creator of The Simpsons, to uncover how its orchestrated theme music is a homage to classic TV of the past. Themes to Z Cars, Bagpuss, Game of Thrones, Mastermind and Grandstand are also celebrated

programme.title: Barbra Streisand: Becoming an Icon 1942-1984
programme.desc: Actress and singer Barbra Streisand grew up in working-class Brooklyn, dreaming of escape from her tough childhood. A stellar student, she resisted the pressure to go to college as her sights were firmly set on Broadway. She was determined to become an actress and landed her first role aged 16, but it was two years later, when she started to sing, that her career took off. This documentary charts her rise to stardom and the remarkable achievements of her early career

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob meets Bette Midler in New York, almost five decades after she began her rise from nightclub act to Hollywood star. At the age of 20, the woman who would become known as 'the Divine Miss M' went from working in a pineapple-canning factory in Honolulu, Hawaii, to a role in the 1967 Broadway hit Fiddler on the Roof, and has never looked back. She recalls those early days, as well as the nightclubs and bathhouses of the 1970s, and how she finally made it to the top of the film industry, the combination of a raucous wit and soulful voice making her an outrageous but captivating all-round entertainer

programme.title: Girls in Bands at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of performances by some of the best female musicians in rock, from the 1970s to the present day. Artists featured include American rock group Fanny, the powerful vocals of Elkie Brooks on Vinegar Joe's Proud to Be a Honky Tonk Woman, the poetry of Patti Smith's Horses and upbeat energy of the Go-Go's on We Got the Beat

programme.title: Kate Bush at the BBC 1979
programme.desc: Performances by Kate Bush, recorded in 1979. Songs include Gymnopedie No 1, Symphony in Blue, Them Heavy People, Madrigal, December, Wedding List, Egypt, Ran Tan Waltz, Man with the Child in His Eyes and Don't Push Your Foot on the Heartbreak. Guest singer Peter Gabriel, who joins the host in a rendition of Another Day and sings Here Comes the Flood

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on April 26, with performances by artists including Pat & Mick, Natalie Cole, Unique 3, Paula Abdul, Jesus Jones, Phil Collins, Tongue ‘n' Cheek, Madonna and Bruce Dickinson

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on May 3, with performances by artists including Sinitta, Soul II Soul, Morrissey, the Adventures of Stevie V, BBG featuring Dina Taylor, Bruce Dickinson, Adamski, Madonna and Emma

programme.title: The Truth About Christmas Carols
programme.desc: Composer Howard Goodall uncovers the often turbulent and uncomfortable relationship between carols and the celebration of Christmas. Though around since the 12th century, the hymns have only been a feature of services for the past 150 years, as the Church believed them to be pagan or of no value. They have been used as secret codes, saved from the brink of extinction and subject to state censorship. The programme includes performances by folk star Bella Hardy, the Purbeck Village Choir and the Truro Cathedral Choir, who recreate the very first Service of Nine Lessons and Carols

programme.title: Francesco's Italy Top to Toe
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto concludes his travels in the south of Italy, where his family has lived for 500 years. He explores the rock caves of Matera, attends Nocera's Easter celebrations and finds out about collapsible houses designed to help the residents avoid paying taxes. He also visits Sicily, where he takes a trip to Mount Etna and the catacombs of Palermo

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, the unflappable traveller visits Mexico, the USA and Canada. He roams the frozen wastes of Alaska, pauses at the Mexican border, does time in Alcatraz, hangs out with lumberjacks and hitches a lift with the US Coast Guard as he tries to get back to the exact spot from where he started all those miles before

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: The team edges nearer to identifying a suspect with the help of a fingerprint lifted from Thor's photograph and CCTV footage from the shopping mall, but this success is jeopardised by a leak to social media that risks putting further lives in danger. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Kata is suspended from duty for losing her gun, while Arnar questions the hospitalised Steinthor, who strenuously denies killing his friend Tomas. Kata gets an intriguing phone call from Hakon, who has discovered something in the basement at Valhalla. In Icelandic

programme.title: Operation Iceberg
programme.desc: Part two of two. Chris Packham, Helen Czerski, Andy Torbet, Chris van Tulleken and Doug Allan continue their adventures in the Arctic, boarding an unpredictable iceberg to determine whether the impact of waves on the ice or the warming temperature of the surrounding water causes them to decay. Andy and Doug dive into the sea, medic Chris Van Tulleken looks at the physiological reactions of the human body in extreme immersion and Chris Packham takes to the skies to conduct a census documenting just how many polar bears could be living on the berg

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Pressure mounts as Tommy's uncooperative mother cannot be held much longer without the supply of evidence. Niels Thormod's name resurfaces in the investigation as it transpires a private detective he hired hoping to find something more than the police has gone missing. Lillian is hard-pressed to solve the case and it seems there is an inside source leaking information. Saga is called to Sweden when a young man wanting to confess to killing his father, has a connection to Henrik's family's past. Crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Thure Lindhardt

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The homeless girls discover the identity of the killer following an encounter with psychologist Niels Thormod, and try to get in touch with Henrik to pass on what they know. However, they are forced to flee for their lives when the murderer comes looking for them. Saga finds a match for the killer's fingerprint and seems to have the case solved. But as she sets out to finally bring him to justice, a last minute revelation involving a seemingly insignificant detail changes everything. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: What is it like when your mum becomes your dad? Twelve-year-old Tilly’s mum came out as transgender when she was 7 years old. We follow Tilly as she shows us what her life is like now. Having just moved up to secondary school, Tilly hasn't told her new friends about her family as she is uncertain as to when will be the right time to tell them. When her birthday party comes up, Tilly has a decision to make - will she explain to her friends that Jack is trans or will she make up a story to buy herself more time? To help her decide, Tilly meets Nat and Faith, who both have trans dads and help her feel more confident about sharing her story. And when younger brother Mal experiences some mean comments at school, new friend Nat offers to give a talk to his class.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are going to Westman Islands, Iceland, where the world’s largest colony of Atlantic puffins gathers every year. Lars loves these tough little birds and explains how, although they spend most of their lives flying out to sea and fishing, they return to land each year to lay their eggs. When the baby birds, also called pufflings, eventually hatch, they fly out towards the ocean. But the light from nearby towns often confuses them about where to go, leading local children and adults to form 'puffin patrols' to rescue the pufflings. Lars is excited to help out but is upset when he discovers Glitch, also a fan of puffins, is already well-known on the islands for his skills as a puffin rescuer. Although Glitch offers him a place in his puffin patrol, Lars is determined to outdo Glitch with a patrol of his own - but when he recruits a clumsy Grimbot, things take a turn for the worse...

programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in Australia, exploring the Wolfe Creek Crater, also known as Kandimalal. While Ubercorn explains how the crater was formed by a meteorite, Foz searches it for fragments of the space rock, as objects from space often contain precous metals. But soon he has competition in the form of Glitch, who races Foz to find an object that both of their metal detectors pick up - which turns out to be a rusty spoon! In the end, neither of them manage to find any pieces of meteorite, prompting Glitch to send the Grimbots to drag a huge space rock to Earth to help him! Can the Go Jetters prevent this huge disaster before it's too late?

programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are beside a river. They watch a fisherman make his rod longer and longer and longer. The fisherman casts out the line and then settles down for a nap. programme.desc: Lars and Kyan swim through the shallows of Crystal River in Florida. Kyan tests his new swimming fin, while Lars watches a manatee and her baby cruise by. Xuli is water-skiing further downstream in the open waters of Crystal Bay, while Foz and Ubercorn watch on from Jet Pad. Grandmaster Glitch is taking the tourist family on a mermaid spotting tour of Crystal River aboard his Grimmer-boat. As Glitch heads off in search of real mermaids, he accidentally startles the mother manatee. Ubercorn realises the manatee will be in danger from the speeding boats and from the cold deep water. The Go Jetters need to mount a manatee rescue quickly. While Xuli attempts to stop the speedboats, Foz launches the Vroomster. The baby manatee has become attached to Kyan, so Lars suggests he remains with her, while Lars and Foz go to save the mother. Kyan is not happy at having to play baby-sitter.

programme.desc: The twins get a new rabbit, but struggle to pick a name for him

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Okai has invited his friends over for a traditional Ghanaian meal made using okra. He works very hard in the kitchen to make a lovely tasting, colourful stew for them to enjoy

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Zainab shows us that stars and landmarks can help us navigate our environment

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat: Rockpool Tales
programme.desc: Old Jack and Salty are surprised when they find Jack's old car hooter. Jack remembers the hooter being useful when he was playing on the beach with Little Ernie and Little Sue as it makes a great sound. Bernard Cribbins stars

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley meets Farmer Billy and learns all about Brussels sprouts. Both he and co-presenter JB Gill visit a very special family run farm and share a festive poem. JB discovers how Christmas trees are grown with Farmer Clive and finds out about mistletoe and holly

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: In this episode we follow Rose as she celebrates Christmas with her family. Rose and her family visit the garden centre, where they find lots of festive decorations which they purchase alongside a Christmas tree. Once they arrive back at home, they all work together to decorate the house. After putting up the tree and leaving a sign for Santa in the window, Rose's parents take her for a magical experience: visiting Santa Claus in his grotto. Here, Rose tells Santa all about how well behaved she has been this year. Before leaving, Rose says goodbye to Santa and his reindeers, which are all outside in the snow. When Christmas Eve finally arrives, Rose attends a Christmas carol service with her family. Here they learn about the story of Jesus and sing some carols together. They head home to put on their festive pyjamas before going to bed. The next morning it's Christmas Day, and the family wake up to their presents under the tree. After the family give each other their gifts, they sit do

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old Maia has a big family weekend planned. First, she visits her baby cousin Jessica. They enjoy playing peek-a-boo together and Maia even helps to feed her. Later, Dad's family arrive for a traditional Zambian meal. Maia helps prepare the food with her Aunty and then they all dress up in special Zambian clothes to eat

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Rory visits a large dog who is visiting the vet for a check-up before her weight loss class

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day explains about the challenges faced by the newborns of animals such as wildebeest, penguins and caiman, and how they rely on their parents to get them around

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Animation following the adventure of a group of animals living on a patchwork blanket

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Bobble builds a hotel for the Scuttlies, but it's too well made! They make Scooper Dooper, who transforms it, and builds a bigger one for the Olobobs to have a holibob too

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble makes breakfast for Tumble Ted. Meanwhile, Justin and his friends are collecting eggs for their breakfast

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Although they have a wonderful submarine, the Kraken is very clever and can camouflage itself. Our friends use a sandwich to draw the animal out and get some great photos.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Felix and Nutkin quarrel, the Squirrel Camp is soon in chaos, and it falls to Peter to help them make up before disaster strikes. Adventures with the animal and his friends

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetties race to save the White Cliffs of Dover

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo needs to find different-coloured leaves to complete her nature tick-book, but she can’t find a green one. What will she do? Luckily, Gran Gran has a Gran Gran Plan that involves a special leaf rubbing!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: In this winter special, Ferne travels to Norway to find out about animals that live in very cold climates. She meets Joar and his herd of reindeer and together they visit a polar park. They find out how animals adapt to live in cold winter conditions.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this episode, Grace shows off three amazing machines that help with farming! Grace drives a massive compost turner, shows off a machine that makes hay bales and then picks some tasty carrots in a carrot harvester!

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome Bella, Olive, Jack, Isobel, Jonas, Leo to The Baby Club.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Pink Bird runs wild in a department store, buying lots of beautiful things

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Kari is scared of playing her trumpet in front of the Treasure Champs, but Barry reminds her how he had courage to buy her trumpet in the first place. Barry also shows courage by going into a dark cave where a funny creature lives. Storyteller Ben Langley visits a zoo to tell the story of Daniel and the Lion's den

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: An African folk tale about why ostriches stick their heads in the ground

programme.title: CBeebies Peter Pan
programme.desc: The CBeebies presenters star in a musical adaptation of JM Barrie's tale. With Katy Ashworth, Katrina Bryan, Cerrie Burnell, Andy Day, Richard David-Caine, Joseph Elliott, Justin Fletcher, Phil Gallagher, Gemma Hunt, Chris Jarvis, Rebecca Keatley, Ella Kenion, Steven Kynman, Pui Fan Lee, Cat Sandion, Sid Sloane and Alex Winters

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady's poetry records inspire a snowy train ride to the mountains

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The stubborn little sister asks for a pair of ice skates, but her dad worries she will not want them for long

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: A day out in the park shows Tee that it can be more fun to share the playground rides with his friends than to always go first

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Igglepiggle dances with Upsy Daisy before everybody joins in at the gazebo. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Adil Ray reads Penguin and Pinecone, by Salina Yoon

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin catches up with an ambitious project to create a forever home in Crewe. Plus, a former stables in Derby and a studio flat in London's Paddington

programme.desc: Secret filming is the undoing of an opportunist attempting to exaggerate his personal injury claim, and insurers deal with a rear-end shunt that has plenty of twists and turns

programme.desc: The volunteers from Northumbria Blood Bikes who transfer life-saving supplies between hospitals, and the Northern Powergrid team tackling power cuts through the night

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant help two teams at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, while Charlie Ross finds out about a collection of medieval church paintings

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al fails to put the brakes on his relationship with Lily, Karen's good deed backfires and Sid is called to treat a stab wound - but the teenage victim refuses to reveal the culprit

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a budget of £375,000 to play with, Ginny Buckley joins in the search for a rural home in north Devon with adjoining land for animals. During her time in the county, Ginny joins the harvest at a traditional Devonshire orchard where over 70 of the country's heritage apple varieties are grown

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, Sarah Moore rescues a vintage pram in the hope of generating a profit for its owners. A windbreaker and an old ottoman are also spruced up

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers get to work on a satchel owned by a prisoner of war, a pub clock with an intriguing past and a sea-serpent pen holder. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby prepares to announce who has won the Lucy Beale Award, but he still doesn't know who to give the money to, so Ian tells him he knows who Lucy would have wanted to win. Tina and Shirley are eager to see Ian's downfall, Phil and Sharon's divorce gets ugly and a furious Ben will stop at nothing to find out who informed on the Mitchells

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets the Sweet family in Weston-super-Mare, whose three children have all been diagnosed with multiple rare illnesses and will soon be full-time wheelchair-users. It may also affect their speech, fine motor skills and vision. All three children already struggle with basic normal life at home, despite the love and care of their parents Cat and Chris. So the DIY SOS team - plus hundreds of local workers - are here to give them the four walls they need

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The strongest chefs compete in the last quarter-final, where they are challenged to create a dish that captures the essence of the forest, making the most of ingredients from meat to foraging produce such as mushrooms and berries. They then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Grace Dent and Jay Rayner, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Bath, inviting Minister for Universities Michelle Donelan, Shadow Police and Fire Services Minister Sarah Jones, Professor of Experimental Medicine at Imperial College Peter Openshaw, economist and journalist Liam Halligan and chef and restaurant owner Tom Kerridge to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties in Motherwell in Scotland and South Normanton, Derbyshire, and a piece of land in Stevenage in Hertfordshire

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers. Here, the team at one of the country's biggest shopping malls installs a three-metre tall reindeer for Christmas shoppers. In Tynemouth the biggest fish catch of the year is landed with only hours to process the catch, while refuse collectors work hard to get Newcastle's streets clean and waste recycled before dawn breaks

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles steps back in time to the 'Georgian days', revisiting the show's episodes in which six teams bought some of the best Georgian items. Eric takes a look at how the items sold at auction, and shares some top tips on what viewers should look out for when scouring the fairs

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers get to grips with a protective cover for mobile phones, heated mittens and a frying pan able to pour sauces. Presented by Lucy Alexander

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win a £10,000 jackpot, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a pair of returning expats find their ideal country home in North Wales for a budget of £350,000. Away from hunt, the presenter heads out with a local fisherman to gather wild mussels using an ancient method that has been used on Wales' northern coast for 200 years

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues more items from a recycling centre in Altrincham. He turns to woodworker Bruce Faulseit to restyle a discarded pub table, while furniture restorer Katie Keat is tasked with bringing a mid-century table back to its best. A tired badminton supplies box is Jay's creative project, but will he succeed in turning trash into cash?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea is delighted to work on a 1950s toy bike gifted to the owner's brother by comedy duo Laurel and Hardy, during a performance in their home town. The pedals have seized up and rust has taken hold of the body work. Other items worked on include a pair of beloved boxing gloves and a shattered ostrich egg cigarette box. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic great Hannah Cockcroft, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey, Olympic hockey champion Kate Richardson-Walsh and Toronto Wolfpack forward Jon Wilkin join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker for the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It seems everyone is out to get Ian, with Max planning his revenge and an angry Ben confronting his brother. Just then the police arrive and Ben presumes they are here to speak to him - but Ian is the one they arrest. Bobby starts to see his dad for who he really is, and Mick tells Tina that his depression is nothing to do with losing the Vic

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: The level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows as comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return for a third time to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts the quiz, inviting author and presenter Dr Hannah Fry and stand-up comedian Phil Wang to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian takes to the streets of Cwmbran to learn the business of selling houses. Gavin, Rhod's mentor, believes happy people sell, placing extra pressure on the grumpy comedian. Can Rhod's unique approach cope with the high-octane sales environment and make him employee of the month, or will he have the home buyers running scared?

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets actor and writer Stephen Fry, who is promoting Troy, his latest reworking of the Greek myths and Mamma Mia star Amanda Seyfried discusses new David Fincher film Mank. Back to the Future star and Parkinson's disease activist Michael J Fox chats about his memoir No Time Like the Future: An Optimist Considers Mortality, Nina Sosanya talks about her new Christmas drama, TV chef Jamie Oliver explains how his new book 7 Ways manages to breathe new life into staple ingredients and comedian Tom Allen talks about his memoir No Shame. Plus, Dolly Parton makes a special appearance performing the seaonal staple I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus

programme.title: Dark Land: Hunting the Killers
programme.desc: George Shotton wed Mamie Stuart in 1918, despite already being a married man. Though suspected of Mamie's disappearance a year later, he evaded conviction. It wasn't until 1961, when divers discovered human remains in a cave near Caswell Bay, that he was posthumously sentenced. The team looks at a 1934 incident known as the Brighton Trunk Murder, in which a dismembered body was found split between two trunks. Given the similarities between this murder and that of Mamie Stuart, the team believes that Shotton could be a prime suspect for this murder too

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The Drag Race UK tour reaches Birmingham. The clock strikes 8pm and the fans are ready for their girls. The lights are down, the curtain is up, but the queens or nowhere to be seen. Will a miscue spell disaster?

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The cold and flu season has the queens struggling to make it through the tour. First, Divina succumbs then Blu, with Sum Ting - who's been battling a cough since the start - losing her voice. With no backing tracks available, it remains to be seen whether she be able to pull off her solo

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by special guest Tim Peake. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes featuring one of her favourite ingredients - the anchovy. She prepares a creamy dressing for the fish, served with bitter leaves and walnuts, and then creates an elixir, combining silvery salted anchovies with garlic and oil. This not only makes an excellent dip for red chicory, but also the perfect accompaniment to a platter of sliced seared steak. Nigella creates an anchovy pasta dish of spaghetti with chard and chilli, and also makes a lemon blossom cocktail and a lemon and elderflower pudding

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Chelsea v Leeds United and Liverpool v Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the opening semi-final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. After facing Liang Wenbo in the quarter-final, China's Ding Junhui played another of his compatriots at this stage last year, defeating Yan Bingtao to book his place in the final against Scot Stephen Maguire. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special 1990s edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Tamara Beckwith, Saffron Sprackling, Clare Buckfield, John Pickard, Mr Motivator, Diane Youdale, Nick Moran and Vas Blackwood

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Ali and Nevada from London, engaged couple Sean and Alan from Chester, and military drummer Ronnie and his partner Wesley from Aldershot

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: It's Musicals Week on Strictly, meaning that remaining contestants Bill, HRVY, Jamie, JJ, Maisie and Ranvir - minus Clara, who was voted off last week - are keen to dazzle as perform routines inspired by hit shows including Little Shop of Horrors, Phantom of the Opera, Chitty Chitty Bang Bang and A Chorus Line. However, it's also the quarter-final and with the final looming ever closer, the pressure is on. The extravaganza gets under way with a Priscilla-themed routine from the professionals. Presented Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize. Each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund receives a visit from the bloodthirsty Bishop of Bath and Wells, who threatens to do him a mischief with a red-hot poker over the matter of an unpaid loan. As he attempts to raise the £1,000 he needs, he finds himself being constantly summoned to the court by the Queen. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Chelsea v Leeds United at Stamford Bridge, Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium and Burnley v Everton at Turf Moor

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Baltimore Ravens v Dallas Cowboys at M&T Bank Stadium

programme.title: Keeping Rosy (2014)
programme.desc: A businesswoman who has devoted her life to running a media agency is betrayed by her partners. Emotional and angry, she commits an uncharacteristically violent act, and takes increasingly desperate measures to make amends and protect an innocent child from further harm. Thriller, starring Maxine Peake and Blake Harrison

Plus, Professor Hayden Lorimer rediscovers another lost construction in the landscape, and this week it's one man's folly
