programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts the quiz, inviting author and presenter Dr Hannah Fry and stand-up comedian Phil Wang to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: The wedding is put at risk after Denise kicks up a stink and accuses Dave of a heinous crime - flirting with Beverley Macca. As far as she's concerned, the wedding is off. After a talk with parents Jim and Barbara, Denise softens her stance and the family seeks an appropriate way to celebrate the pair's reconciliation. Comedy written by and starring Caroline Aherne and Craig Cash, with Ricky Tomlinson and Sue Johnston

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets actor and writer Stephen Fry, who is promoting Troy, his latest reworking of the Greek myths and Mamma Mia star Amanda Seyfried discusses new David Fincher film Mank. Back to the Future star and Parkinson's disease activist Michael J Fox chats about his memoir No Time Like the Future: An Optimist Considers Mortality, Nina Sosanya talks about her new Christmas drama, TV chef Jamie Oliver explains how his new book 7 Ways manages to breathe new life into staple ingredients and comedian Tom Allen talks about his memoir No Shame. Plus, Dolly Parton makes a special appearance performing the seaonal staple I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic great Hannah Cockcroft, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey, Olympic hockey champion Kate Richardson-Walsh and Toronto Wolfpack forward Jon Wilkin join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker for the quiz

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The Drag Race UK tour reaches Birmingham. The clock strikes 8pm and the fans are ready for their girls. The lights are down, the curtain is up, but the queens or nowhere to be seen. Will a miscue spell disaster?

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The cold and flu season has the queens struggling to make it through the tour. First, Divina succumbs then Blu, with Sum Ting - who's been battling a cough since the start - losing her voice. With no backing tracks available, it remains to be seen whether she be able to pull off her solo

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by special guest Tim Peake. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes featuring one of her favourite ingredients - the anchovy. She prepares a creamy dressing for the fish, served with bitter leaves and walnuts, and then creates an elixir, combining silvery salted anchovies with garlic and oil. This not only makes an excellent dip for red chicory, but also the perfect accompaniment to a platter of sliced seared steak. Nigella creates an anchovy pasta dish of spaghetti with chard and chilli, and also makes a lemon blossom cocktail and a lemon and elderflower pudding

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Chelsea v Leeds United and Liverpool v Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the opening semi-final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. After facing Liang Wenbo in the quarter-final, China's Ding Junhui played another of his compatriots at this stage last year, defeating Yan Bingtao to book his place in the final against Scot Stephen Maguire. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special 1990s edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Tamara Beckwith, Saffron Sprackling, Clare Buckfield, John Pickard, Mr Motivator, Diane Youdale, Nick Moran and Vas Blackwood

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Ali and Nevada from London, engaged couple Sean and Alan from Chester, and military drummer Ronnie and his partner Wesley from Aldershot

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: It's Musicals Week on Strictly, meaning that remaining contestants Bill, HRVY, Jamie, JJ, Maisie and Ranvir - minus Clara, who was voted off last week - are keen to dazzle as perform routines inspired by hit shows including Little Shop of Horrors, Phantom of the Opera, Chitty Chitty Bang Bang and A Chorus Line. However, it's also the quarter-final and with the final looming ever closer, the pressure is on. The extravaganza gets under way with a Priscilla-themed routine from the professionals. Presented Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize. Each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund receives a visit from the bloodthirsty Bishop of Bath and Wells, who threatens to do him a mischief with a red-hot poker over the matter of an unpaid loan. As he attempts to raise the £1,000 he needs, he finds himself being constantly summoned to the court by the Queen. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Chelsea v Leeds United at Stamford Bridge, Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium and Burnley v Everton at Turf Moor

programme.title: Sportscene

programme.title: The NFL Show

programme.title: Keeping Rosy (2014)
programme.desc: A businesswoman who has devoted her life to running a media agency is betrayed by her partners. Emotional and angry, she commits an uncharacteristically violent act, and takes increasingly desperate measures to make amends and protect an innocent child from further harm. Thriller, starring Maxine Peake and Blake Harrison

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin catches up with an ambitious project to create a forever home in Crewe. Plus, a former stables in Derby and a studio flat in London's Paddington

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Secret filming is the undoing of an opportunist attempting to exaggerate his personal injury claim, and insurers deal with a rear-end shunt that has plenty of twists and turns

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: The volunteers from Northumbria Blood Bikes who transfer life-saving supplies between hospitals, and the Northern Powergrid team tackling power cuts through the night

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant help two teams at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, while Charlie Ross finds out about a collection of medieval church paintings

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al fails to put the brakes on his relationship with Lily, Karen's good deed backfires and Sid is called to treat a stab wound - but the teenage victim refuses to reveal the culprit

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a budget of £375,000 to play with, Ginny Buckley joins in the search for a rural home in north Devon with adjoining land for animals. During her time in the county, Ginny joins the harvest at a traditional Devonshire orchard where over 70 of the country's heritage apple varieties are grown

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, Sarah Moore rescues a vintage pram in the hope of generating a profit for its owners. A windbreaker and an old ottoman are also spruced up

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers get to work on a satchel owned by a prisoner of war, a pub clock with an intriguing past and a sea-serpent pen holder. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby prepares to announce who has won the Lucy Beale Award, but he still doesn't know who to give the money to, so Ian tells him he knows who Lucy would have wanted to win. Tina and Shirley are eager to see Ian's downfall, Phil and Sharon's divorce gets ugly and a furious Ben will stop at nothing to find out who informed on the Mitchells

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets the Sweet family in Weston-super-Mare, whose three children have all been diagnosed with multiple rare illnesses and will soon be full-time wheelchair-users. It may also affect their speech, fine motor skills and vision. All three children already struggle with basic normal life at home, despite the love and care of their parents Cat and Chris. So the DIY SOS team - plus hundreds of local workers - are here to give them the four walls they need

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The strongest chefs compete in the last quarter-final, where they are challenged to create a dish that captures the essence of the forest, making the most of ingredients from meat to foraging produce such as mushrooms and berries. They then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Grace Dent and Jay Rayner, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Bath, inviting Minister for Universities Michelle Donelan, Shadow Police and Fire Services Minister Sarah Jones, Professor of Experimental Medicine at Imperial College Peter Openshaw, economist and journalist Liam Halligan and chef and restaurant owner Tom Kerridge to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties in Motherwell in Scotland and South Normanton, Derbyshire, and a piece of land in Stevenage in Hertfordshire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Anonymous tip-offs reveal an outrageous plan to cash in after a flood ruins a house and, when a missing watch turns up, it is not the good news that the claimant expected

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers. Here, the team at one of the country's biggest shopping malls installs a three-metre tall reindeer for Christmas shoppers. In Tynemouth the biggest fish catch of the year is landed with only hours to process the catch, while refuse collectors work hard to get Newcastle's streets clean and waste recycled before dawn breaks

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles steps back in time to the 'Georgian days', revisiting the show's episodes in which six teams bought some of the best Georgian items. Eric takes a look at how the items sold at auction, and shares some top tips on what viewers should look out for when scouring the fairs

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win a £10,000 jackpot, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a pair of returning expats find their ideal country home in North Wales for a budget of £350,000. Away from hunt, the presenter heads out with a local fisherman to gather wild mussels using an ancient method that has been used on Wales' northern coast for 200 years

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues more items from a recycling centre in Altrincham. He turns to woodworker Bruce Faulseit to restyle a discarded pub table, while furniture restorer Katie Keat is tasked with bringing a mid-century table back to its best. A tired badminton supplies box is Jay's creative project, but will he succeed in turning trash into cash?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea is delighted to work on a 1950s toy bike gifted to the owner's brother by comedy duo Laurel and Hardy, during a performance in their home town. The pedals have seized up and rust has taken hold of the body work. Other items worked on include a pair of beloved boxing gloves and a shattered ostrich egg cigarette box. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Witness History
programme.desc: For this special, women-focused edition of Witness History, the BBC's Ciru Muriuki in Nairobi brings us five moments from the recent past as told to us by the women who were there. We hear from the daughter of the world's first female prime minister. We also learn about the fight for equal rights for women at the Western Wall in Jerusalem and the campaign for the right to take the contraceptive pill in Japan. Plus, Nasa's female space pioneers and one of the crew members of the Discovery Shuttle mission that put into orbit one of the most remarkable scientific instruments of all time, the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: The Firing Line
programme.desc: 2020 has been heavily defined by the Covid-19 pandemic, which has led to social disruption around the world and the deepest economic recession since the Great Depression. The year has also seen a record number of entries for the Rory Peck Awards. The awards pay tribute to an international field of freelance journalists around the world who have continued to capture some of the most dramatic and important news and current affairs stories - working in difficult and dangerous circumstances. The Firing Line, presented by Yalda Hakim, features the finalists and the winners of the awards.

programme.title: Lennon Remembered: A Reporter's Journal
programme.desc: Tom Brook marks the fortieth anniversary of former Beatle John Lennon's murder in New York on 8 December 1980. His untimely death was a slaying that shocked millions of fans around the world. In Lennon Remembered: A Reporter's Journal, the BBC's Tom Brook, the first British journalist to report live from the site of the New York shooting, takes stock of Lennon's life and the legacy of his music. Brook recounts what happened on the night Lennon died when he returned to his home, the Dakota apartment building on Manhattan's Upper West Side, with his wife Yoko Ono, and he hears from some of Lennon's closest confidantes as well as younger Lennon fans in America today. Drawing on a wealth of archive material, the programme includes Tom Brook's interview with the former Beatle's widow Yoko Ono, one of the first she gave, in which she discussed Lennon's life and death. The programme assesses John Lennon's powerful legacy 40 years on and asks whether his messages of love and peace are still re

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Emily, Roddy, Dannii, Morgan, Josh, Tess, Faye, Andy and Lewis are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Robert, Alison, Amy and Paul work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Priya is annoyed with Jai, Manpreet reveals Meena's history to David, and Brenda returns but is annoyed

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen takes to the witness box. Steve goes missing ahead of Oliver's memorial. Roy suggests a way of halting Ray's proposed demolition

programme.title: Griff's Great Australian Adventure
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones takes his first tandem parachute jump from 12,000ft, to view an internationally renowned Australian landmark - Uluru, which is taller than the Eiffel Tower. He then catches a train from Alice Springs, stopping at a siding in the middle of the Outback near the opal mining town of Coober Pedy, where he fossicks through mullock with a local 'noodler'. Lastly, Griff travels 1,535kms south to Adelaide to experience just how extraordinary Australia is, meeting Barossa bakers, a snake catcher, and ending aboard the oldest passenger train in the country

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya and Sally give evidence at Yasmeen's trial. Leanne says a final goodbye to her son. Sarah's careless talk puts Peter on high alert

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: It's the final, and after three weeks of facing their fears, coping with hunger and battling the elements, Ant and Dec reveal who will be crowned King or Queen of the Castle

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Harry's Heroes: Euro Having a Laugh
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp takes his team of ex-England players on a European tour, as they prepare for a rematch against the German Legends on their own soil. They begin in France at a self-catering hotel in the quiet medieval town of Provins, where Harry wants them to be on a detox and cook their own healthy food, but they don't even get through the first night without hitting the beers and ending up at the curry house

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha creates an American-themed meal with Christian Stevenson - aka DJ BBQ. On the menu are Maryland home fries for breakfast, buttermilk fried chicken for lunch, Christian's Smash Burger and tomato pie for dinner, with Nadia's Washington apple crisp for dessert

programme.title: Scrambled!

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: The Mystery Inc. team is back and just as courageous, adventurous, humorous, and obsessed with cases as ever. When Scooby and Shaggy compete in a fast food eating competition, they meet their ultimate challenge against comedian Jim Gaffigan while contending with the Spectral Speedster, who is trying to stop the contest from finishing.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin feels the other Titans are spending too much time together so he encourages them to spend some time alone

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by singer Sophie Ellis-Bextor, while chefs Ravneet Gill and James Tanner provide their own recipes. Plus, James provides a masterclass in lentils and embarks on a food adventure at historic Goodwood House

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare some Christmas dishes, including ham in parsley sauce, and curried puffs and potato bombas. Lisa also prepares her grandmother's recipe for traditional mincemeat, which can be used in both mince pies and a festive frangipane layer cake

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James joins up with fellow chef Lisa Allen to explore Whitby, in James' home county of North Yorkshire. James pops into his favourite fish and chip shop before cooking tikka mackerel buns with fennel and apple salad for the crowds at Whitby bandstand. Lisa, meanwhile, pays a visit to Michelin-starred restaurant The Black Swan, to see how its huge kitchen garden provides almost all of the produce used in the kitchen

programme.title: Back to the Future Part II (1989)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly travels to the 21st century, where he has to save his children from a terrible fate. However, this proves to have unforeseen consequences when the future version of arch enemy Biff sees a chance to rewrite history for his own benefit, leaving Marty stuck in a twisted parallel universe. Sci-fi comedy sequel, starring Michael J Fox and Christopher Lloyd

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: The final round of the obstacle-course challenge arrives, as Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara invite the most successful participants from throughout this series to return to the course and put their strength, speed and agility to the test once again

programme.title: Celebrity Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a star-studded edition of the game show, with teams consisting of Emmerdale stars Charley Webb and Matthew Wolfenden, The Chase's Jenny Ryan and singer Megan McKenna, and DJs Melvin Odoom and Rickie Haywood-Williams racing down the aisles to win a £10,000 jackpot for the charity of their choice

programme.title: Catchphrase: Catchiest Moments
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern presents a third compilation of some of his favourite moments from the quiz show, from the funniest answers to the strangest. Featuring previously unseen footage, and giving viewers a chance to play along at home as contestants make unexpected attempts to Say What They See

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a special Emmerdale edition, as Mark Charnock, Charley Webb, Matthew Wolfenden and Amy Walsh pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams presents a compilation of TV clangers from the past year's programmes. Featuring live on-air blunders from Stormzy, Robbie Williams, Craig Revel Horwood, Jonathan Ross, and Ant & Dec, as well as a series of outtakes starring Will.i.am and Jeremy Clarkson. Plus, two toddlers run rings around Kay Burley, and Vicky Pattison forgets the name of the show she's presenting

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined on tonight's show by guests Michael Palin and Kylie Minogue, as well as his co-stars on the next series of The Masked Singer, Rita Ora, Davina McCall and Mo Gilligan

programme.title: Through the Keyhole
programme.desc: Keith Lemon hosts a revival of the classic game show. Today's panellists are Dani Dyer, Martin Kemp and Paddy McGuinness, who are given a tour of three mystery celebrity homes, and are challenged to guess the identities of the famous occupants

programme.title: The Keith & Paddy Picture Show
programme.desc: Keith Lemon teams up with Paddy McGuinness to remake Steven Spielberg's 1993 dinosaur-based classic Jurassic Park. The duo are helped by Sian Gibson, Sally Phillips, George Potts, Shaun Ryder and Bernie Clifton to reimagine the story of genetically engineered dinosaurs that escape from an eccentric tycoon's safari park and go on the rampage

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Having made it through to the quarter-finals, two families go head to head for a place in the semi-finals of the tournament, aiming to move a step closer to winning the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Family and a holiday of a lifetime. Hosted by Anne Hegerty

programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team are in Weston-super-Mare, Somerset, where dealers Mark Stevens, Aiden Pass and Alison Chapman lay down their cash for items of interest including a gold guinea, US election ephemera and a stamp collection

programme.title: Winning Combination

programme.desc: Keith Lemon is back to take more famous faces out shopping and other fun non-shopping related activities whilst having a chat about their life and career. Prepare for gossip, laughs and shocking revelations. This week, Keith gets a yoga lesson from presenter Laura Whitmore and explores a wildlife park with football pundit and presenter Chris Kamara.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Lyndsey threatens to end things with Alan when he refuses to give her a key to the house, while Walden, Herb and Billy commiserate about their failed relationships. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher, Jon Cryer and Courtney Thorne

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jake returns from the base with his girlfriend Tammy, and asks Walden for money so he can propose to her. However, the teenager has misunderstood his relationship with the tattoo shop owner, who has several children and just wants a bit of fun with no strings attached. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmonds offers his unique insights on some Guinness World Records, including the world's shortest and longest cats, the highest waterfall jump and the longest underwater kiss

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show

programme.title: Dress to Impress

programme.desc: Keith Lemon hosts the near-the-knuckle comedy quiz, with radio DJ Roman Kemp, actor Richard Blackwood, Top Gear's Chris Harris, and Claire and H from Steps joining in the fun with team captains Laura Whitmore and Emily Atack

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After falling in love with a woman called Jess, Brian receives the devastating news that she has cancer so he proposes marriage. Return of the animated comedy

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian's marriage to Jess is not what he envisioned, so at Peter's suggestion he lets himself go. Meanwhile, Chris and Stewie take up vaping

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger and Klaus have a secret affair after accidentally going on an internet date together, while Steve tries to make himself ill to get out of a fitness test at school

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger runs off in disguise as a Julia Roberts character to a small town after Stan hurts his feelings. Steve plans a meeting when he finds out that Jeff and Barry have never met

programme.title: Crossing Swords
programme.desc: The annual Kraken Week celebration is disrupted by protestors who kidnap the king and queen, as well as Patrick's parents

programme.title: Crossing Swords
programme.desc: The king and queen worry Princess Blossom might be targeted by a serial killer at an annual festival, so they assign Patrick to protect her

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna gets into an argument with Arianna at a silent auction, so Cleveland defends his wife's honour by throwing eggs at her adversary's house. He decides to do the same to all the people who have offended his family, but problems arise when he is accused of a murder he did not commit. Guest starring Arianna Huffington

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland begins to socialise with Dr Fist, but a well-meaning attempt to get in touch with the medic's old friends reveals a potentially life-threatening secret. Meanwhile, Junior and Rallo open a mobile catering business at school, but their enterprise faces competition

programme.title: The Emily Atack Show
programme.desc: Emily takes a deep dive into the theme of friendship, and is joined by some of the most cherished people in her life as she discusses making friends, fall-outs, and dinner parties

programme.title: Bless This House

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid discovers where the money for his birthday party came from. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in order

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Fred collapses with a heart attack just moments away from a memorable victory on the pitch. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: The season of goodwill descends on the Larkin household, and the family celebrates the 'perfick' Christmas. Drama, based on the characters created by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Henry Miller comes to stay with the family, and stirs up tension among Louisa and her children with his strange behaviour and the controversial content of his books. Sven is arrested after the government announces a crackdown on what it considers 'immoral behaviour', and Larry rushes to his aid, acquiring books on fascism and miscarriages of justice from the countess to help him take matters into his own hands. Spiros continues to pine for his wife, and Basil makes a surprise appearance on the island, leaving the family on edge about Aunt Hermione's estate

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rosie is shocked when farmer Simon Langley-Smythe shoots her aunt's dog Snowy while out on a walk, and PC Younger fails to chase down children who have released livestock from the fields. Meanwhile, Carol is concerned when she sees Julie Langley-Smythe nursing a black eye

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic is furious with Chris Tate, Kelly tells Mandy that she still intends to have an abortion, and Sam turns on Zak for never teaching him to read

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah storms to Rachel's house and wants to see Jack. The Glovers talk to Jan's psychiatrist. Ned is apprehensive about the suggestion that Jan can return home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma struggles to free herself in the water, Tricia assures Vera she will still be able to see Brad, and the Platts and Mike are summoned to the hospital

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma is terrified when Don kidnaps her. Vera gives Tricia a hard time about moving out. Chris hopes that Angie will sleep with him. Jim tells Andy not to throw his life away

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the death of a maintenance engineer found shot in the head in the basement of the Bodleian Library. A search of the man's house reveals a stash of valuable volumes and a connection to the local Gamblers Anonymous group, and the detectives go on to expose a scam involving two Oxford academics. Drama, guest starring Neil Pearson and Haydn Gwynne, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: An influx of holiday-makers to Portwenn annoys Martin, and his efforts to point out the dangers of sunbathing to a scantily clad woman prove unpopular. He is also the target of a practical joke in the pub after his fear of blood becomes common knowledge, and grows closer to Louisa when one of her pupils has a serious accident that lands him in hospital. Drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The body of a man killed by a single gunshot wound is discovered on the moors and a burnt-out truck containing the remains of a deer is found a mile away. Joe is convinced the case is a poaching war gone wrong, but for Vera, the truth is never that straightforward. Delving into the victim's story, she unearths a sad childhood in the country and a troubled life in the city, scraping by as a novelist. Following the recent death of his grandfather, he returned to Northumberland after a 15-year absence to sell his inheritance to a wealthy couple - but pulled out of the deal at the last minute. Could his death be linked to a dispute over land? Brenda Blethyn and David Leon star in the murder mystery

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: It's turnaround day on the Regal Princess, when the crew has to help 3,300 passengers check out with 10,000 pieces of luggage and then welcome the new batch - with just a few hours to clean and prepare 1,800 rooms. As the staff members work against the clock, hotel manager Dirk makes his usual inspections - and will not tolerate any slip in standards. Meanwhile, dancer Dulcie is feeling homesick, and front-desk supervisor Herbert is under pressure to locate some missing luggage and have it delivered at the next port

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: The staff compete to win the coveted title of employee of the month, which carries a top prize of $500, as well as deck privileges, tours and extra time off. Deck hand Rhymond is among those with their eye on the top prize, having braved the elements since 5am to keep the ship looking pristine. Meanwhile, Timothy feels sad about leaving his friends on the front desk to start his new job with the shore excursions team, and engineer Scott is left in charge when his boss goes on leave, with jobs including making drinking water from the sea

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid discovers why Jean is sulking and Sally and Mike will not talk to him. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's new neighbours are offended by Mike's latest sculpture. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Mim sustains an injury, leaving Roger with the responsibility of organising the team teas, and Alex's insecure girlfriend puts in an appearance. Cricketing comedy drama, with Brenda Blethyn and Robert Daws

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Ma Larkin wins a holiday weekend in Paris and Pop decides to make the trip special by treating it as a second honeymoon. Heart-warming drama, starring David Jason and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family meets members of a travelling circus, and gets involved in helping them to drum up business. Larry falls in love with a contortionist, while Margo takes to the ring in person to perform a very dangerous act. Gerry is more concerned with making sure all of the animals featured in the show are well-treated. Spiros cannot bring himself to return to his empty house and begins spending more and more time at the Durrells' home - but the magic of the circus brings him and Louisa closer together

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Harold Beecham angrily accuses his neighbour Roger Parslow when his dog dies from poisoning, and his refusal to co-operate with the police lands him in prison, where a tragedy occurs that puts PC Younger's career on the line. Meanwhile, Carol confronts a herbalist who was treating Harold, and the people of Aidensfield are intent on impressing a visiting radio journalist. Richard Todd and Ralf Little guest

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris tells Kim that Kelly has moved into the nursery flat. Zoe tries to smooth things over with the Windsors. Roy wants to make a big thing about Jan's homecoming

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy thinks Biff should be told that he is really the father of Kelly's baby. Jed Outhwaite causes trouble in the hospital again, and Sarah argues with Rachel

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera hopes Terry will settle down now he has a family. Jack is upset to hear that Don is in a psychiatric hospital. Ray tells Tricia she is making a mistake if she stays with Terry

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Terry finds Tricia's affection suffocating. Kevin confides in Chris that Natalie keeps making a play for him. Mavis worries that Wilf wants a relationship with her

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The death of Oxford don RG Cole leads to the shady underworld of unlicensed boxing and the involvement of academic Richard Helm. Investigations reveal the dead man's student Jack Roth was involved in a complex love triangle, and when the young man's rival is found dead in an apparent suicide, Lewis suspects foul play and discovers an old connection to Helm - along with a link to his former mentor Morse. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. Back from sick leave, Morse uncovers new evidence concerning the year-long investigation into the murder of Yvonne Harrison, but has to cope with playing second fiddle to long-standing sidekick Lewis - and there are questions about whether he is fit enough to handle the case. Kevin Whately co-stars, with James Grout, Jesse Birdsall, Clare Holman and Paul Freeman

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Fifty-year-old Deborah Ozturk has spent years longing to find her birth mother and thank her for the sacrifice she made when she gave her up as a baby. Meanwhile, 74-year-old Ray Jones from West Sussex realised as an adult that there was a void in his life, after he was separated from his mother and younger brother at the age of two. However, during his search for his family the previous year, he accessed his case file and is now haunted by a missed opportunity. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the mysterious death of businessman John Shearwood, found floating in the water under the Gateshead Millennium Bridge when he was supposed to be on a golfing trip to Ireland. The man's wife Stella and brother Luke are baffled to discover he was working as an informant for HM Revenue and Customs. Chief Revenue investigator Owen Preece (guest star Robert Glenister) reveals John's business manager Mark Donovan has been running an alcohol scam through their haulage firm and the deceased had agreed to offer information in exchange for immunity from prosecution. Was his cover blown? Owen doesn't think so, and Mark has an alibi - unlike John's wife. Brenda Blethyn stars, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A middle-aged Oxford graduate is found murdered, setting in motion an investigation that leads Lewis and Hathaway to one of the university's most senior dons, whose respectable exterior conceals a strange past. Before long a second death occurs, and it becomes apparent that both victims shared a secret - and that someone is out for revenge. Starring Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Anna Massey, Clare Holman and Sian Thomas

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Captain Perrin throws a huge party to celebrate the ship's first birthday, with executive chef Shaun creating a giant cake that is so heavy it takes 10 men to carry - and they soon come unstuck when they cannot fit the ship-shaped sponge into the lift. Passengers Norman and Maria are looking forward to attending the lavish formal party, although they will be going nowhere unless front-desk supervisor Herbert can locate their missing luggage, which contains his tuxedo and her party frock. Dancer Dulcie faces her staff appraisal, and a visit from her boyfriend helps with her homesickness

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Romance is in the air for casino host Cecilia and assistant maitre d' Alberto - and she already has clear ideas about the size of her engagement ring. Things are not so happy for waitress Valeria, whose relationship ended when her boyfriend left the ship and now a close friend is leaving too. However, it isn't long until a new beau catches her eye. Timothy came on board after splitting from his partner of four years - but he has little time to worry about his broken heart in his new job with the shore excursions team, especially when he runs into hot water with a couple of misplaced passengers

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is determined to listen to classical music, but someone keeps interrupting. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When Sid receives a bonus, the family are quick to remind him how helpful they all are

programme.title: Carry On at Your Convenience (1971)
programme.desc: Production at toilet manufacturers WC Boggs is brought to a halt when an over-eager trade union representative calls a strike. However, worker unrest is quickly quelled by a jolly trip to the seaside. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Cope, Hattie Jacques, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Bernard Bresslaw

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The inspector and his sidekick DS Hathaway investigate the murder of a talented maths student. The prime suspect is the victim's ex-boyfriend, who is convinced his uncle has set him up, but old case papers belonging to Morse reveal the boy's family is hiding a dark secret. Laurence Fox and Rebecca Front co-star alongside Kevin Whately

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective suspects a connection between the death of a student with secret plans for advancement and the killing of well-known criminal Nicky Turnball, who was in Oxford to speak at the Union. As Lewis and Hathaway eliminate suspects from a long list of the crook's enemies, the investigation brings him into contact with old flame Diane - who also happens to be Turnball's wife. Kevin Whately stars, with Laurence Fox and Gina McKee

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When the detective is called to investigate what appears to be a freak accident at Lady Matilda's College, he uncovers a potential link between a series of peculiar incidents across Oxford. Despite Thursday's scepticism, he becomes convinced the accidents are the result of foul play. Lady Matilda's is in the middle of a referendum campaign to decide whether or not the college should become co-educational - and when a woman is attacked while walking alone on a towpath, Endeavour and Thursday try to find the assailant before they strike again. Drama, starring Shaun Evans

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is brought in to investigate when a former Met police officer is stabbed to death outside a nightclub in Newcastle. The team initially suspects a crime of passion after it emerges the victim had recently rekindled his affair with his childhood sweetheart, whose husband has a history of violence. However, Vera uncovers evidence pointing to a more complex case involving inside information and the hostile takeover of a brewery. What did the deceased find out that cost him his life? Guest starring Jill Halfpenny, Christine Bottomley, Ralph Ineson and Liam Cunningham

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky masters the fine art of living well on nothing a year, proving that a respectable public appearance is all you need to get credit. Her flirtation with Lord Steyne at least provides a reliable source of income, but the Sedley family are less fortunate. With her finances in tatters, a devastated Amelia is forced to send her son to live with the Osbournes. Becky rises to the very top of high society, even securing a royal audience - but Steyne expects something in return for his patronage, and when Rawdon is accosted by bailiffs, he seizes his chance

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is left two large packing cases in an uncle's will - but they come with a terrible curse

programme.desc: Alma struggles to free herself in the water, Tricia assures Vera she will still be able to see Brad, and the Platts and Mike are summoned to the hospital

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma is terrified when Don kidnaps her. Vera gives Tricia a hard time about moving out. Chris hopes that Angie will sleep with him. Jim tells Andy not to throw his life away

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the death of a maintenance engineer found shot in the head in the basement of the Bodleian Library. A search of the man's house reveals a stash of valuable volumes and a connection to the local Gamblers Anonymous group, and the detectives go on to expose a scam involving two Oxford academics. Drama, guest starring Neil Pearson and Haydn Gwynne, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: An influx of holiday-makers to Portwenn annoys Martin, and his efforts to point out the dangers of sunbathing to a scantily clad woman prove unpopular. He is also the target of a practical joke in the pub after his fear of blood becomes common knowledge, and grows closer to Louisa when one of her pupils has a serious accident that lands him in hospital. Drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The body of a man killed by a single gunshot wound is discovered on the moors and a burnt-out truck containing the remains of a deer is found a mile away. Joe is convinced the case is a poaching war gone wrong, but for Vera, the truth is never that straightforward. Delving into the victim's story, she unearths a sad childhood in the country and a troubled life in the city, scraping by as a novelist. Following the recent death of his grandfather, he returned to Northumberland after a 15-year absence to sell his inheritance to a wealthy couple - but pulled out of the deal at the last minute. Could his death be linked to a dispute over land? Brenda Blethyn and David Leon star in the murder mystery

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: It's turnaround day on the Regal Princess, when the crew has to help 3,300 passengers check out with 10,000 pieces of luggage and then welcome the new batch - with just a few hours to clean and prepare 1,800 rooms. As the staff members work against the clock, hotel manager Dirk makes his usual inspections - and will not tolerate any slip in standards. Meanwhile, dancer Dulcie is feeling homesick, and front-desk supervisor Herbert is under pressure to locate some missing luggage and have it delivered at the next port

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: The staff compete to win the coveted title of employee of the month, which carries a top prize of $500, as well as deck privileges, tours and extra time off. Deck hand Rhymond is among those with their eye on the top prize, having braved the elements since 5am to keep the ship looking pristine. Meanwhile, Timothy feels sad about leaving his friends on the front desk to start his new job with the shore excursions team, and engineer Scott is left in charge when his boss goes on leave, with jobs including making drinking water from the sea

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah storms to Rachel's house and wants to see Jack. The Glovers talk to Jan's psychiatrist. Ned is apprehensive about the suggestion that Jan can return home

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rosie is shocked when farmer Simon Langley-Smythe shoots her aunt's dog Snowy while out on a walk, and PC Younger fails to chase down children who have released livestock from the fields. Meanwhile, Carol is concerned when she sees Julie Langley-Smythe nursing a black eye

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid discovers why Jean is sulking and Sally and Mike will not talk to him. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's new neighbours are offended by Mike's latest sculpture. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Mim sustains an injury, leaving Roger with the responsibility of organising the team teas, and Alex's insecure girlfriend puts in an appearance. Cricketing comedy drama, with Brenda Blethyn and Robert Daws

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Ma Larkin wins a holiday weekend in Paris and Pop decides to make the trip special by treating it as a second honeymoon. Heart-warming drama, starring David Jason and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family meets members of a travelling circus, and gets involved in helping them to drum up business. Larry falls in love with a contortionist, while Margo takes to the ring in person to perform a very dangerous act. Gerry is more concerned with making sure all of the animals featured in the show are well-treated. Spiros cannot bring himself to return to his empty house and begins spending more and more time at the Durrells' home - but the magic of the circus brings him and Louisa closer together

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Harold Beecham angrily accuses his neighbour Roger Parslow when his dog dies from poisoning, and his refusal to co-operate with the police lands him in prison, where a tragedy occurs that puts PC Younger's career on the line. Meanwhile, Carol confronts a herbalist who was treating Harold, and the people of Aidensfield are intent on impressing a visiting radio journalist. Richard Todd and Ralf Little guest

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris tells Kim that Kelly has moved into the nursery flat. Zoe tries to smooth things over with the Windsors. Roy wants to make a big thing about Jan's homecoming

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy thinks Biff should be told that he is really the father of Kelly's baby. Jed Outhwaite causes trouble in the hospital again, and Sarah argues with Rachel

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera hopes Terry will settle down now he has a family. Jack is upset to hear that Don is in a psychiatric hospital. Ray tells Tricia she is making a mistake if she stays with Terry

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Terry finds Tricia's affection suffocating. Kevin confides in Chris that Natalie keeps making a play for him. Mavis worries that Wilf wants a relationship with her

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The death of Oxford don RG Cole leads to the shady underworld of unlicensed boxing and the involvement of academic Richard Helm. Investigations reveal the dead man's student Jack Roth was involved in a complex love triangle, and when the young man's rival is found dead in an apparent suicide, Lewis suspects foul play and discovers an old connection to Helm - along with a link to his former mentor Morse. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. Back from sick leave, Morse uncovers new evidence concerning the year-long investigation into the murder of Yvonne Harrison, but has to cope with playing second fiddle to long-standing sidekick Lewis - and there are questions about whether he is fit enough to handle the case. Kevin Whately co-stars, with James Grout, Jesse Birdsall, Clare Holman and Paul Freeman

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Fifty-year-old Deborah Ozturk has spent years longing to find her birth mother and thank her for the sacrifice she made when she gave her up as a baby. Meanwhile, 74-year-old Ray Jones from West Sussex realised as an adult that there was a void in his life, after he was separated from his mother and younger brother at the age of two. However, during his search for his family the previous year, he accessed his case file and is now haunted by a missed opportunity. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the mysterious death of businessman John Shearwood, found floating in the water under the Gateshead Millennium Bridge when he was supposed to be on a golfing trip to Ireland. The man's wife Stella and brother Luke are baffled to discover he was working as an informant for HM Revenue and Customs. Chief Revenue investigator Owen Preece (guest star Robert Glenister) reveals John's business manager Mark Donovan has been running an alcohol scam through their haulage firm and the deceased had agreed to offer information in exchange for immunity from prosecution. Was his cover blown? Owen doesn't think so, and Mark has an alibi - unlike John's wife. Brenda Blethyn stars, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A middle-aged Oxford graduate is found murdered, setting in motion an investigation that leads Lewis and Hathaway to one of the university's most senior dons, whose respectable exterior conceals a strange past. Before long a second death occurs, and it becomes apparent that both victims shared a secret - and that someone is out for revenge. Starring Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Anna Massey, Clare Holman and Sian Thomas

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Captain Perrin throws a huge party to celebrate the ship's first birthday, with executive chef Shaun creating a giant cake that is so heavy it takes 10 men to carry - and they soon come unstuck when they cannot fit the ship-shaped sponge into the lift. Passengers Norman and Maria are looking forward to attending the lavish formal party, although they will be going nowhere unless front-desk supervisor Herbert can locate their missing luggage, which contains his tuxedo and her party frock. Dancer Dulcie faces her staff appraisal, and a visit from her boyfriend helps with her homesickness

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Romance is in the air for casino host Cecilia and assistant maitre d' Alberto - and she already has clear ideas about the size of her engagement ring. Things are not so happy for waitress Valeria, whose relationship ended when her boyfriend left the ship and now a close friend is leaving too. However, it isn't long until a new beau catches her eye. Timothy came on board after splitting from his partner of four years - but he has little time to worry about his broken heart in his new job with the shore excursions team, especially when he runs into hot water with a couple of misplaced passengers

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is determined to listen to classical music, but someone keeps interrupting. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When Sid receives a bonus, the family are quick to remind him how helpful they all are

programme.title: Carry On at Your Convenience (1971)
programme.desc: Production at toilet manufacturers WC Boggs is brought to a halt when an over-eager trade union representative calls a strike. However, worker unrest is quickly quelled by a jolly trip to the seaside. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Cope, Hattie Jacques, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Bernard Bresslaw

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The inspector and his sidekick DS Hathaway investigate the murder of a talented maths student. The prime suspect is the victim's ex-boyfriend, who is convinced his uncle has set him up, but old case papers belonging to Morse reveal the boy's family is hiding a dark secret. Laurence Fox and Rebecca Front co-star alongside Kevin Whately

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective suspects a connection between the death of a student with secret plans for advancement and the killing of well-known criminal Nicky Turnball, who was in Oxford to speak at the Union. As Lewis and Hathaway eliminate suspects from a long list of the crook's enemies, the investigation brings him into contact with old flame Diane - who also happens to be Turnball's wife. Kevin Whately stars, with Laurence Fox and Gina McKee

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When the detective is called to investigate what appears to be a freak accident at Lady Matilda's College, he uncovers a potential link between a series of peculiar incidents across Oxford. Despite Thursday's scepticism, he becomes convinced the accidents are the result of foul play. Lady Matilda's is in the middle of a referendum campaign to decide whether or not the college should become co-educational - and when a woman is attacked while walking alone on a towpath, Endeavour and Thursday try to find the assailant before they strike again. Drama, starring Shaun Evans

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is brought in to investigate when a former Met police officer is stabbed to death outside a nightclub in Newcastle. The team initially suspects a crime of passion after it emerges the victim had recently rekindled his affair with his childhood sweetheart, whose husband has a history of violence. However, Vera uncovers evidence pointing to a more complex case involving inside information and the hostile takeover of a brewery. What did the deceased find out that cost him his life? Guest starring Jill Halfpenny, Christine Bottomley, Ralph Ineson and Liam Cunningham

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky masters the fine art of living well on nothing a year, proving that a respectable public appearance is all you need to get credit. Her flirtation with Lord Steyne at least provides a reliable source of income, but the Sedley family are less fortunate. With her finances in tatters, a devastated Amelia is forced to send her son to live with the Osbournes. Becky rises to the very top of high society, even securing a royal audience - but Steyne expects something in return for his patronage, and when Rawdon is accosted by bailiffs, he seizes his chance

programme.desc: Sid is left two large packing cases in an uncle's will - but they come with a terrible curse

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon visits the West Indies, but his excursion turns into an investigation into the murder of an old friend. Sixties drama, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Part one of two. In preparation for an invasion of Wales, King John orders all food from the local community to be collected in order to feed his army. Robin and his band are unwilling to stand by and let their people starve, and form a resistance with the villagers of Wickham

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Harriet poses as a journalist in an attempt to track a notorious criminal trying to muscle in on London's organised crime scene, and soon becomes embroiled in the machinations of a crafty French jewel thief. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry watches over the home of a 1960s rock star, while he is away playing a season in Las Vegas. However, it transpires the veteran musician is bankrupt when bailiffs turn up at the property to re-possess his belongings. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is given a deadly new rifle for testing, but the weapon is promptly stolen. To make matters worse, he discovers he is being stalked by an old friend who has embarked on a vendetta. Vicki Michelle ('Allo 'Allo!) makes an early screen appearance, alongside Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A frustrated housewife supplies Regan with the names of the gang who recently ransacked a bookmakers - including that of her husband, a notorious robber. However, he has a cast-iron alibi, so proving his guilt proves harder than anticipated. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar poses as a Russian secret police chief to catch an assassin who has claimed the lives of 13 intelligence agents. Starring Roger Moore, Glynn Edwards and Burt Kwouk

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Merrie Men are captured by the evil Guy of Gisburne, which gives Gulnar the opportunity to launch his new creation on an unsuspecting world. Richard O'Brien guest stars in the final episode of the fantasy adventure series

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Dempsey finds himself the target of a crazed female admirer. Guest starring Elizabeth Sladen and Suzi Quatro

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's niece is set to be married and he has agreed to supply the wedding limousine - but her big day is placed in jeopardy when he finds he also needs the vehicle to shift a consignment of pornographic magazines. Warren Clarke guest stars, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The brother of a suspected drug-dealer dies while in custody, and his elder sibling directs his powerful lawyer to instigate a committee to investigate CI5's involvement. With the future of the division in question, Bodie and Doyle try to prove the family's drug-trade connection and the real reason behind the man's death. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's holiday is disrupted by the escape of a violent psychopath from prison. Tim Cook, dubbed 'The Leopard' by the press has vowed murderous revenge on the detective inspector for his earlier capture, and has friends on the outside prepared to harbour him while he plans his attack. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts the quiz, in which a team of a family of five answers questions about top 10 lists then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: On the Road
programme.desc: The team go off-roading in a pair of mud buggies, drive a 1.6million pound Ferrari speedster in Milan and put the new Tesla Model 3 through its paces. They also head to Scotland in an all-weather Lamborghini and try out the latest pick-up trucks

programme.title: Shaun of the Dead (2004)
programme.desc: An unambitious 29-year-old man recently dumped and trapped in the job from hell decides to get his life back on track. Unfortunately, he has to put his plans on hold when zombies rise from their graves and stalk the streets of London to feast on the living. Comedy horror, starring Simon Pegg, Nick Frost, Kate Ashfield, Dylan Moran, Lucy Davis, Bill Nighy and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces action from the semi-final at Wembley Stadium, which was decided by a penalty shootout. Bob Wilson presented the coverage, with analysis from Glenn Hoddle, Jack Charlton and John Barnes, and commentary by Brian Moore and Kevin Keegan

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Reports of attacks in a remote part of Ukraine take extreme angler Jeremy Wade on a hunt for an elusive lake monster, and to the site of the world's worst nuclear accident - Chernobyl's reactor number four. Can he reveal the culprit and deliver it to the scientists searching for evidence of mutant species?

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A selection of nine dart finishes from ITV Sport's darts coverage through the years. Every darts player strives to produce a perfect nine dart finish, but under the bright lights of the TV cameras, this pinnacle of darting prowess is even more impressive.

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Oli Bell presents another top-class day of National Hunt Racing from Sandown and Aintree. The feature race of the day is the 3.00 Tingle Creek Chase at Sandown over two miles. Chief supporting races on the card at Sandown include the 1.50 Henry VIII Novices' Chase and two very competitive handicaps. At Aintree there are two races over the Grand National Fences for the first time this season, including the Becher Chase, where some of the leading fancies for the National itself will be in action. Plus, there are some top-class chasers in action in the 2.40 Many Clouds Chase. Analysis from Mick Fitzgerald and Luke Harvey, reporting from Rishi Persad, Alice Plunkett, Matt Chapman and Sally Ann Grassick and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Flash Gordon (1980)
programme.desc: American football quarterback Flash Gordon and journalist Dale Arden find themselves aboard a rocket ship designed by scientist Dr Zarkov, who intends to travel across space to locate the source of recent natural disasters. The three arrive on the planet Mongo, where they soon learn that the attacks are the responsibility of evil emperor Ming the Merciless, who is planning to destroy the Earth. Sci-fi comic-strip adventure, with Sam J Jones, Max von Sydow, Brian Blessed, Melody Anderson, Topol, Timothy Dalton, Ornella Muti, Peter Wyngarde and Mariangela Melato. Featuring music by Queen

programme.title: The Saint

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's niece is set to be married and he has agreed to supply the wedding limousine - but her big day is placed in jeopardy when he finds he also needs the vehicle to shift a consignment of pornographic magazines. Warren Clarke guest stars, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The brother of a suspected drug-dealer dies while in custody, and his elder sibling directs his powerful lawyer to instigate a committee to investigate CI5's involvement. programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's holiday is disrupted by the escape of a violent psychopath from prison. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: Shaun of the Dead (2004)

Comedy horror, starring Simon Pegg, Nick Frost, Kate Ashfield, Dylan Moran, Lucy Davis, Bill Nighy and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Property developer Diane's Margaret Thatcher costume causes some conflict, but there is some brief harmony when a busker arrives to lead the guests in singing a Beatles song

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Company administrator Louise Emmerson serves a Mediterranean menu inspired by her former mother-in-law, featuring Madrid chicken and aubergine bake. However, the guests are not impressed by the food, and Sam Appleby stokes the simmering feud between Diane Jones and the host, much to the shock of the other diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Property developer Diane Jones hosts the final dinner party in Leeds, and her guests have high expectations for the evening. She rustles up Greek filo pastries and cod in Italian sauce as well as entertaining her diners with Brazilian dancing on the lawn, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Fifteen to One

programme.title: Frasier
